reasoning: The question asks whether Wei Yi's Elo rating on Wikipedia will increase by at least 1% (approx. 27.5 points) from its level on Nov 9, 2025. Wikipedia's "FIDE rankings" page updates only once a month alongside FIDE's official monthly publications. Therefore, the rating on Nov 16 will be identical to Nov 9, making a 1% increase impossible (0.02 minimum bound). To increase by 27.5 points by Dec 9 or Feb 7, Wei Yi (whose K-factor is 10) would need to score 2.75 points above expectation. This translates to an astronomical single-tournament performance (like +6 or +7 score in a very strong closed event) within a 1- or 3-month timespan. As an established low-variance Super-GM, achieving this exact spike over this brief and arbitrary timeframe is highly unlikely. We assign roughly a 5% baseline chance for a massive 1-month outlier performance, and 9% for a sustained hot streak across 3 months.
